title: AWS Transfer Manager, Concurrency, and Migration · Language Lens: Transfer Resource Budget
summary: A large-transfer service starts new work on deprecated s3/manager, accepts unstable defaults, multiplies concurrency per request, and cannot bound memory, descriptors, or partial failures. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the transfer resource budget increment in transfer-manager adoption and resource budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Bound aggregate part concurrency, buffers, descriptors, NICs, DNS behavior, progress callbacks, and caller admission across simultaneous transfers.
Use directory transfer with explicit traversal, path mapping, symlink, overwrite, partial-success, cancellation, and failure policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Transfer Resource Budget · Theory Transfer Resource Budget
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 5496 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to adopt transfermanager 0.3.2 while rejecting deprecated use and bounding aggregate concurrency; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Transfer Resource Budget contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Transfer Resource Budget · Theory Transfer Resource Budget.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bound aggregate part concurrency, buffers, descriptors, nics, dns behavior, progress callbacks, and caller admission across simultaneous transfers. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Transfer Resource Budget · Theory Transfer Resource Budget, keep Transfer Resource Budget connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Build Transfer Resource Budget evidence · Theory Transfer Resource Budget
steps.instruction:

Browser code must not import AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, fuzz, race, leak, controlled AWS, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
